prompt:

Lean files live under: /<repo-root>/CausalSmith/CausalSmith/Substrate/ProductLossMonotoneCoupling
Target declarations: CausalSmith.Substrate.ProductLossMonotoneCoupling.instIsProbabilityMeasure_unifOI, CausalSmith.Substrate.ProductLossMonotoneCoupling.monotoneOn_quantile, CausalSmith.Substrate.ProductLossMonotoneCoupling.aemeasurable_quantile_unifOI, CausalSmith.Substrate.ProductLossMonotoneCoupling.quantile_map_uniform

## Instructions
File: CausalSmith/Substrate/ProductLossMonotoneCoupling/PIT.lean. Fill the 4 sorries. DO NOT change any statement/signature; only replace `sorry` (private helper lemmas allowed). Keep it building with no errors.

Context: `unifOI := volume.restrict (Set.Ioo (0:ℝ) 1)`. `quantile`, `quantile_le_iff {τ x}(0<τ)(τ<1): quantile μ τ ≤ x ↔ τ ≤ cdf μ x`, `quantile_mono {τ τ'}(0<τ)(τ'<1)(τ≤τ'): quantile μ τ ≤ quantile μ τ'` come from `Causalean.Stat` (open). `cdf = ProbabilityTheory.cdf`.

1. `instIsProbabilityMeasure_unifOI`: `⟨measure_univ = 1⟩`; `unifOI Set.univ = volume (Ioo 0 1)` via `Measure.restrict_apply_univ`, then `Real.volume_Ioo` gives `ENNReal.ofReal (1-0)=1`.
2. `monotoneOn_quantile`: `intro a ha b hb hab; exact quantile_mono ha.1 hb.2 hab` (ha:a∈Ioo 0 1 so ha.1:0<a; hb.2:b<1).
3. `aemeasurable_quantile_unifOI`: quantile is monotone on Ioo 0 1 (lemma 2) and unifOI = volume.restrict that measurable set. Search Mathlib for a MonotoneOn ⇒ measurability/aemeasurability lemma (`MonotoneOn.aemeasurable`, `MonotoneOn.measurableOn`, or restrict a `Monotone.measurable` via `MeasurableSet.aemeasurable`); use `lean_leansearch`/`lean_loogle` for the exact name. `measurableSet_Ioo` gives the set.
4. `quantile_map_uniform`: prove `unifOI.map (quantile μ) = μ` via `MeasureTheory.Measure.ext_of_Iic` (both are finite/prob measures). For each `x`, `(unifOI.map (quantile μ)) (Iic x) = unifOI ((quantile μ)⁻¹' Iic x)` by `Measure.map_apply` (needs `aemeasurable_quantile_unifOI` — may need `Measure.map_apply₀`). On the interior, `(quantile μ)⁻¹' Iic x ∩ Ioo 0 1 = {u∈Ioo 0 1 | u ≤ cdf μ x}` by `quantile_le_iff`; its `volume.restrict (Ioo 0 1)` mass is `ENNReal.ofReal (cdf μ x)` since `0 ≤ cdf μ x ≤ 1` (`cdf` valued in [0,1]); and `μ (Iic x) = ENNReal.ofReal (cdf μ x)` (find the `cdf`↔`Iic`-mass lemma, e.g. `ProbabilityTheory.cdf` / `StieltjesFunction` measure-of-Iic, or `Measure.ext_of_Iic` directly comparing to μ via `cdf_eq_iff`). Note `cdf_eq_iff (μ ν)` may give a slicker route: show the pushforward has cdf equal to `cdf μ`. Use whichever is cleaner; verify with `lean_diagnostic_messages` after.

Use the lean-lsp MCP tools to check your proof (diagnostics/goal). When done, output a SHORT plain-text report: which decls you closed, which remain, and any blocker. No JSON required.
